recommendation.title: Full rest — TSB still caution-zone negative
recommendation.reasoning: TSB is -22.9 — better than yesterday's -24.3, but still below the -15 caution threshold, so recovery outranks any bonus movement. HRV came in at 77ms, up 4% from a 74ms baseline, and sleep score was 89 (6.5h) — both solid — but the device's own readiness score (44) confirms fatigue is still sitting underneath the good recovery numbers. Wednesday has no session in the plan between Tuesday's easy run and Thursday's first marathon-pace intervals, so this is already a rest day by design — no change needed, just protect it.
recommendation.sessionDetail: No scheduled training today. Full rest — let TSB climb back toward neutral before Thursday's 3×5' at marathon pace (4:15–4:20/km).
recommendation.bikeNote: U-Bahn both ways today — TSB is still in caution territory (-22.9), no reason to add cycling load on what's supposed to be a genuine rest day.

sessions.lesson: Duration hit the 35-minute target almost exactly, but pace (5:91/km) ran clearly slower than the prescribed 5:00–5:15/km while HR (139) still sat near the top of the easy zone — this week's fatigue is making easy pace cost more than it should.
Swim class stayed exactly at the low, 'don't push' aerobic effort it called for — avg HR 111 is comfortably easy, in line with active recovery.
Unscheduled Sunday session with no GPS distance and a moderate 116bpm avg HR — this is the already-flagged apartment-move exertion (second straight day of moving), not a running workout.

Distance and duration overran the 8km/45min prescription by roughly 55%, and avg HR 154 sat well into tempo effort rather than the easy pace it was supposed to be — the single biggest driver of this week's deep-negative TSB.
Pace (5.69/km) looked like an easy effort, but avg HR 147 crossed into tempo territory above the ~142bpm ceiling — the first of three sessions this week to run hotter than prescribed.

recentActivity.analysis: TSS spiked hard on Saturday's long-run overrun (76.8, versus a plan built for something closer to 35) then settled back to base-phase norms (32–37) through Sunday's move exertion, Monday's swim class, and today's easy run. Three of these five sessions (Jul 9, 11, 14) show avg HR running well above the ~142bpm easy ceiling despite on-target or even slow pace — a recurring pattern, not a one-off — which tracks with TSB bottoming out near -33 mid-week before recovering to -22.9 today.
